skill.name: ML Engineer
skill.description: Build production ML systems with PyTorch 2.x, TensorFlow, and modern ML frameworks. Implements model serving, feature engineering, A/B testing, and monitoring.
skill.summary: Expert ML engineer for production machine learning systems, model serving, and ML infrastructure.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis found 0 files with executable content and computed risk score of 0/100. The SKILL.md file contains only markdown documentation and AI assistant instructions for ML engineering tasks. No security concerns identified.

content.user_title: Build Production ML Systems with Expert Guidance
content.value_statement: Deploying machine learning models to production requires expertise in serving, monitoring, and infrastructure that many teams lack. This skill provides battle-tested patterns for building reliable, scalable ML systems using modern frameworks like PyTorch 2.x and TensorFlow.
actual_capabilities: Design production-ready ML serving architectures with PyTorch 2.x, TensorFlow 2.x, and TorchServe
Implement feature stores using Feast, Tecton, or cloud-native solutions for batch and real-time features
Build distributed training pipelines with PyTorch DDP, Horovod, and DeepSpeed for multi-GPU workloads
Create model monitoring systems for data drift, model drift, and performance degradation detection
Develop A/B testing frameworks with statistical testing, gradual rollouts, and multi-armed bandits
Optimize inference performance through quantization, batching, caching, and hardware acceleration
limitations: Provides guidance and code patterns but does not execute ML training or inference directly
Requires access to actual ML infrastructure (GPU clusters, cloud platforms) for deployment
Model performance depends on data quality and domain-specific tuning beyond this skill's scope
Cloud-specific implementations require appropriate AWS, GCP, or Azure credentials and setup

use_cases.title: Real-time Recommendation System
use_cases.description: Design a high-throughput recommendation engine handling 100K predictions per second with Redis caching and model serving via TorchServe.
use_cases.target_user: ML engineers building consumer-facing recommendation features

use_cases.title: ML Pipeline Automation
use_cases.description: Build end-to-end ML pipelines with Apache Airflow or Kubeflow that automate data processing, training, validation, and deployment.
use_cases.target_user: Data platform teams implementing MLOps practices

use_cases.title: Model Performance Monitoring
use_cases.description: Implement comprehensive monitoring with Prometheus and Grafana to track data drift, prediction latency, and business metrics in production.
use_cases.target_user: ML platform engineers responsible for production model reliability

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: ML Model Deployment Basics
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a trained PyTorch model saved as model.pth. Guide me through deploying it as a REST API using FastAPI and Docker. Include health checks, input validation, and basic logging.

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Feature Store Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a feature store architecture for our e-commerce recommendation system. We need both batch features (user purchase history) and real-time features (session activity). Compare Feast vs Tecton for our use case.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Distributed Training Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: We need to train a 2B parameter transformer model on 8xA100 GPUs. Recommend a distributed training strategy using PyTorch FSDP or DeepSpeed. Include gradient checkpointing, mixed precision, and communication optimization.

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Production ML Monitoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a comprehensive monitoring system for our fraud detection model serving 10K requests/second. Include data drift detection, model performance tracking, alerting thresholds, and automated rollback triggers.

output_examples.input: Design a model serving architecture for image classification with 50ms latency SLA
output: Recommended architecture using TorchServe with GPU instances
Request batching configuration for throughput optimization
Redis layer for prediction caching on repeated inputs
Auto-scaling policy based on queue depth and latency metrics
Circuit breaker pattern for graceful degradation during failures

output_examples.input: How do I implement A/B testing for model comparison
output: Traffic splitting strategy with sticky sessions for user consistency
Statistical power calculation for detecting 2% improvement
Guardrail metrics to monitor for negative side effects
Sequential testing approach with early stopping criteria
Sample size estimation based on baseline conversion rate

best_practices: Always implement comprehensive input validation and data quality checks before model inference to catch drift early
Use infrastructure as code (Terraform, CloudFormation) for reproducible ML infrastructure deployments
Design for graceful degradation with fallback models and circuit breakers to maintain service during failures
anti_patterns: Deploying models without monitoring for data drift or performance degradation leads to silent failures
Hardcoding model paths or hyperparameters in application code instead of using model registries
Running training and inference on the same infrastructure causes resource contention and unpredictable latency

faq.question: Which ML frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: Primary support for PyTorch 2.x and TensorFlow 2.x. Also covers JAX/Flax for research workloads, scikit-learn and gradient boosting libraries for classical ML, and Hugging Face Transformers for LLM applications.

faq.question: Can this skill help with cloud-specific ML services?
faq.answer: Yes. Provides guidance for AWS SageMaker, Azure ML, GCP Vertex AI, and Databricks ML. Includes infrastructure as code templates and best practices for each platform.

faq.question: Does this skill help with model optimization for edge deployment?
faq.answer: Yes. Covers TensorFlow Lite, PyTorch Mobile, and ONNX Runtime for edge devices. Includes quantization, pruning, and distillation techniques for resource-constrained environments.

faq.question: What monitoring tools are recommended for production ML?
faq.answer: Recommends Prometheus and Grafana for infrastructure metrics, Evidently AI or WhyLabs for ML-specific monitoring, and custom business metric dashboards. Includes alerting configuration guidance.

faq.question: How does this skill handle model versioning and rollback?
faq.answer: Covers MLflow Model Registry, DVC, and Git LFS for versioning. Provides strategies for blue-green deployments, canary releases, and automated rollback based on performance thresholds.

faq.question: Can this skill help with distributed training setup?
faq.answer: Yes. Provides configurations for PyTorch DDP, FSDP, Horovod, and DeepSpeed. Covers multi-node setup, gradient accumulation, mixed precision training, and communication optimization.
